title: Hartogia craspedata
intro: Hartogia craspedata, also known as Kola tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and edible seeds. The health benefits of Hartogia craspedata include its ability to act as a stimulant, improve cognitive function, and provide antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include caffeine, theobromine, and other xanthines, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hartogia craspedata is commonly used in traditional medicine and is also used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being a key ingredient in some commercial energy drinks and supplements.
taxonomy: Hartogia craspedata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the madder family. It is a member of the tribe Palicoureeae, which includes plants native to tropical regions. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, with over 13,000 known species.
